Module com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6
Package com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6.model

Class Celda

java.lang.Object
com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6.model.Celda
public class Celda extends Object
Represents a single cell in the 6x6 Sudoku board.

Each cell stores its current value (1 to 6, or 0 if empty), its position within the grid, whether it is a fixed clue (pre-filled and non-editable), and whether it currently violates a Sudoku rule (used for visual feedback).

This class belongs to the model layer of the MVC architecture and must remain independent of any JavaFX or UI-related code.

    Column index of the cell within the board (0-based).

    Indicates whether the cell currently violates a Sudoku rule.

    Maximum valid value a cell can hold (inclusive).

    VALOR_VACIO
    Constant representing an empty cell.

    • fila

      private final int fila
      Row index of the cell within the board (0-based).

    • columna

      private final int columna
      Column index of the cell within the board (0-based).

    • valor

      private int valor
      Current value of the cell. Zero means the cell is empty.

    • esFija

      private boolean esFija
      Indicates whether the cell is a fixed clue and therefore non-editable.

    • tieneError

      private boolean tieneError
      Indicates whether the cell currently violates a Sudoku rule.

  • Constructor Details

    • Celda

      public Celda(int fila, int columna)
      Constructs a new empty cell at the specified position.
      Parameters:
      fila - the row index (0-based, must be between 0 and 5)
      columna - the column index (0-based, must be between 0 and 5)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if row or column are out of bounds

  • Method Details

    • obtenerFila

      public int obtenerFila()
      Returns the row index of this cell.
      Returns:
      the row index (0-5)

    • obtenerColumna

      public int obtenerColumna()
      Returns the column index of this cell.
      Returns:
      the column index (0-5)

    • obtenerValor

      public int obtenerValor()
      Returns the current value of this cell.
      Returns:
      the value (1-6) or VALOR_VACIO if the cell is empty

    • establecerValor

      public void establecerValor(int nuevoValor)
      Sets the value of this cell.

      If the cell is marked as fixed, the value cannot be changed and the method will throw an exception. Use esFija() to check beforehand.

      Parameters:
      nuevoValor - the new value (must be 0 or between 1 and 6)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the cell is fixed
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is out of range

    • limpiar

      public void limpiar()
      Clears the value of this cell, setting it back to empty.

      Has no effect if the cell is fixed.

    • estaVacia

      public boolean estaVacia()
      Checks whether this cell is empty.
      Returns:
      true if the cell has no value, false otherwise

    • esFija

      public boolean esFija()
      Checks whether this cell is a fixed clue.
      Returns:
      true if the cell is fixed and cannot be modified

    • marcarComoFija

      public void marcarComoFija(int valorFijo)
      Marks this cell as a fixed clue with the given value.

      Fixed cells cannot be cleared or modified through establecerValor(int).

      Parameters:
      valorFijo - the value to lock into this cell (must be 1-6)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is out of range

    • tieneError

      public boolean tieneError()
      Checks whether this cell currently violates a Sudoku rule.
      Returns:
      true if the cell is in an error state

    • establecerError

      public void establecerError(boolean estadoError)
      Sets the error state of this cell.

      This flag is typically used by the view layer to highlight cells that contain invalid values (for example, with a red border).

      Parameters:
      estadoError - true to mark the cell as invalid, false to clear the error state

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a human-readable representation of this cell, useful for debugging and console testing.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string describing the cell's state
